text.title: Decision Analysis Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every decision-analysis run enforces.

rule.statement: Criteria weights are frozen BEFORE any cell is scored. weights_locked_at timestamp recorded; any weight change resets the run.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc weight tuning is rubric-laundering; pre-registration protects against motivated reasoning.

rule.statement: Every option×criterion cell carries an evidence URL or citation. Cells without evidence are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Evidence forces grounding; unevidenced scores fail audit and inter-rater reliability.

rule.statement: Options list includes an explicit "do nothing" baseline. A decision matrix without a status-quo column is incomplete.

rationale.source: decision theory / Russo & Schoemaker, Decision Traps (1989)

rule.rationale: Without the baseline, framing bias produces false-urgency choices over no-op.

rule.statement: Sensitivity analysis runs ±20% Monte Carlo over weights and reports rank-flip frequency per option pair. Top-2 with >15% flip rate are flagged as unstable.

rationale.source: Monte Carlo sensitivity / Saltelli, Global Sensitivity Analysis (2008)

rule.rationale: Single-point matrix scores hide weight-dependence. Sensitivity surfaces when the winner depends on weight calibration.

rule.statement: Decision record is signed by the named accountable approver with a 1-paragraph rationale citing the matrix outcome AND the sensitivity result.
rule.rationale: Unsigned decisions get retconned. Signed records survive audit and inform retrospectives.

example.title: Cell satisfying r2
example.code: option: vendor-A criterion: total-cost-of-ownership score: 4 evidence: https://internal-docs/vendor-A-tco-2026.pdf#page=12
